prompt:
Ops ticket from Ledgerline billing, priority high. Tuesday morning a customer statement went out stamped DRAFT. The stamp was added hours earlier by a one-off staging re-run somebody kicked off through the review UI — different document, different job, but the customer's export picked it up anyway. While digging through the worker log we found a second flavor of the same weirdness: back in May an analyst used the 'email me a copy' option ONCE, and ever since, every export the worker renders CCs her. Same with the CSV toggle — one job turns it on and it's on for everybody until the next deploy. Restart the worker process and everything is clean again... until the first person customizes a job, and then the bleed starts over.

What makes this maddening is that the ordinary overrides behave perfectly: page size, orientation, copies — set them on one job, later jobs are untouched. It's specifically the stamp list and the delivery block that go sticky across jobs.

The worker-side class is ExportJob in export_job.rb in this directory. Find what's actually wrong and fix it there — keep the public API and the manifest format exactly as they are, no rewrites. test_export_job.rb is the acceptance contract; don't touch it. Done = `ruby test_export_job.rb` green.
